perturbation.original_text: 1. "Confidential Information" ​ means information that relates to the Purpose (as defined below) or that, although not related to such Purpose, is nevertheless disclosed as a result of the parties' discussions in that regard, and that should reasonably have been understood by the party receiving such information (the “Recipient”), because of legends or other markings, the circumstances of disclosure or the nature of the information itself, to be proprietary and confidential to the party disclosing the information (the “Discloser”) or an Affiliate of the Discloser or to a third party. Confidential Information may be disclosed in written or other tangible form (including on electronic media) or by oral, visual or other means. Confidential Information includes all of the following, whether or not reduced to tangible form: software codes and computer programs; trade secrets, patents, patent applications, and copyrights; know-how, processes, research, development, ideas, and inventions (whether patentable or not); formulas and algorithms; technical drawings, schematics, design, diagrams, models, and flow charts; documentation and specifications; databases and materials; financial information and projections; business plans and needs; employee information; customer lists, sales information and forecasts, marketing plans, customer leads, customer information and anticipated markets; and other information of a similar nature, and any other trade secrets or non-public business information belonging or pertaining to either of the parties. "Affiliate" ​ means any person or entity directly or indirectly controlling, controlled by, or under common control with a party.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. "Confidential Information" ​ means information that relates to the Purpose (as defined below). Confidential Information may be disclosed in written or other tangible form (including on electronic media) or by oral, visual or other means. Confidential Information includes only data explicitly marked as confidential and delivered in tangible form. "Affiliate" ​ means any person or entity directly or indirectly controlling, controlled by, or under common control with a party.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Confidential Information' is narrowed to only include data explicitly marked as confidential and delivered in tangible form. This contradicts the original broader definition that included information that should reasonably have been understood as confidential due to the circumstances of disclosure or the nature of the information itself and was no limited to tangible forms. This creates an in-text contradiction because Section 8 refers back to this definition when specifying remedies for unauthorized release of Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. The parties acknowledge that the Confidential Information is unique and valuable and a breach by Recipient of this Agreement will cause irreparable and continuing damage to Discloser for which money damages are insufficient. In the event of a breach or threatened breach of the obligations under this Agreement, Discloser will be entitled to: (1) seek specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief without the necessity of posting a bond, and (ii) indemnification from any loss or harm, including recovery of attorney’s fees, in connection with any breach or enforcement of Recipient’s obligations hereunder or the unauthorized use or release of such Confidential Information. Any such relief will be in addition to and not in lieu of any appropriate relief in the way of monetary damages. Recipient must notify Discloser in writing immediately upon the occurrence of any such unauthorized release or other breach of which it is aware and will assist Discloser in remedying the unauthorized use or disclosure of Discloser’s Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'Confidential Information' to only include data explicitly marked as confidential and delivered in tangible form, whereas the original definition was broader, including information that should reasonably be understood as confidential and not limited to tangible forms. Section 8, the contradicted text, refers back to this definition when specifying remedies for unauthorized release of Confidential Information. Because Section 8 refers to the broader definition that included things not in tangible form, the narrowing of the definition in the changed text creates an in-text contradiction, because the remedies in Section 8 can no longer be applied to types of Confidential Information originally envisioned by the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: 2. Recipient will not use any Confidential Information except to the extent necessary for the internal purpose of discussing, analyzing, and exchanging information about each party’s products, services and other offerings to determine whether the parties may enter into a mutually beneficial business relationship (“Purpose”). Recipient will not disseminate or disclose any Confidential Information to any person, firm, business or governmental agency or department, except as such disclosure is expressly permitted in this Agreement. Recipient will treat all of Discloser’s Confidential Information with the same degree of care as Recipient treats its own Confidential Information, but not less than reasonable care. Recipient will disclose Discloser’s Confidential Information only to those of Recipient’s employees, consultants, and contractors who have a “need to know” the information to assist Recipient with respect to the Purpose and who are legally bound by terms and conditions substantially similar to those terms and conditions applicable to Recipient under this Agreement. Neither party is obligated under this Agreement to purchase from or provide to the other party any service or product. Recipient agrees not to copy or reverse engineer, or attempt to derive the composition or underlying information, structure or ideas of any Confidential Information. No licenses or rights under any patent, copyright, trademark, or trade secret are granted or are to be implied by this Agreement; Recipient does not acquire any right in or to the Confidential Information except the limited right to use it for the Purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Recipient will not use any Confidential Information except to the extent necessary for the internal purpose of discussing, analyzing, and exchanging information about each party’s products, services and other offerings to determine whether the parties may enter into a mutually beneficial business relationship (“Purpose”). Recipient may disseminate or disclose any Confidential Information to any person, firm, business or governmental agency or department, with the prior written consent of Discloser, which will not be unreasonably withheld. Recipient will treat all of Discloser’s Confidential Information with the same degree of care as Recipient treats its own Confidential Information, but not less than reasonable care. Recipient will disclose Discloser’s Confidential Information only to those of Recipient’s employees, consultants, and contractors who have a “need to know” the information to assist Recipient with respect to the Purpose and who are legally bound by terms and conditions substantially similar to those terms and conditions applicable to Recipient under this Agreement. Neither party is obligated under this Agreement to purchase from or provide to the other party any service or product. Recipient agrees not to copy or reverse engineer, or attempt to derive the composition or underlying information, structure or ideas of any Confidential Information. No licenses or rights under any patent, copyright, trademark, or trade secret are granted or are to be implied by this Agreement; Recipient does not acquire any right in or to the Confidential Information except the limited right to use it for the Purpose.
perturbation.explanation: The original text stated: 'Recipient will not disseminate or disclose any Confidential Information to any person, firm, business or governmental agency or department, except as such disclosure is expressly permitted in this Agreement.' , while the changed text states: 'Recipient may disseminate or disclose any Confidential Information to any person, firm, business or governmental agency or department, with the prior written consent of Discloser, which will not be unreasonably withheld.' This creates a contradiction, as the original text says that the recipient can only share if its 'expressly permitted' on the agreement. Meanwhile the new text states they can withold the information with a written consent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Recipient will not use any Confidential Information except to the extent necessary for the internal purpose of discussing, analyzing, and exchanging information about each party’s products, services and other offerings to determine whether the parties may enter into a mutually beneficial business relationship (“Purpose”). Recipient may disseminate or disclose any Confidential Information to any person, firm, business or governmental agency or department, with the prior written consent of Discloser, which will not be unreasonably withheld. Recipient will treat all of Discloser’s Confidential Information with the same degree of care as Recipient treats its own Confidential Information, but not less than reasonable care. Recipient will disclose Discloser’s Confidential Information only to those of Recipient’s employees, consultants, and contractors who have a “need to know” the information to assist Recipient with respect to the Purpose and who are legally bound by terms and conditions substantially similar to those terms and conditions applicable to Recipient under this Agreement. Neither party is obligated under this Agreement to purchase from or provide to the other party any service or product. Recipient agrees not to copy or reverse engineer, or attempt to derive the composition or underlying information, structure or ideas of any Confidential Information. No licenses or rights under any patent, copyright, trademark, or trade secret are granted or are to be implied by this Agreement; Recipient does not acquire any right in or to the Confidential Information except the limited right to use it for the Purpose.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Recipient cannot disseminate Confidential Information unless expressly permitted in the agreement. The changed text allows dissemination with prior written consent from the Discloser. This contradicts the original restriction, because the Discloser can now permit something that previously could only occur if expressly stated in the agreement. The contradicted text is the same text as the changed text, and the contradiction is based on the modification that occurs in the changed text. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Recipient’s obligations under Section 2 will not apply to any of Discloser’s Confidential Information that Recipient can document: (a) was in the public domain at or subsequent to the time the Confidential Information was communicated to Recipient by Discloser through no fault of Recipient; (b) was rightfully in Recipient’s possession free of any obligation of confidence at or subsequent to the time the Confidential Information was communicated to Recipient by the Discloser; (c) was developed by employees or agents of Recipient independently of and without reference to any of Discloser’s Confidential Information; or (d) was communicated by Discloser to an unaffiliated third party free of any obligation of confidence. A disclosure by Recipient of any of Discloser’s Confidential Information (a) in response to a valid order by a court or other governmental body; (b) as otherwise required by law; or (c) necessary to establish the rights of either party under this Agreement will not be considered to be a breach of this Agreement by the Recipient; provided, however, that Recipient provides prompt prior written notice to the Discloser to enable Discloser to seek a protective order or otherwise prevent the disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Recipient’s obligations under Section 2 will not apply to any of Discloser’s Confidential Information that Recipient can document: (a) was in the public domain at or subsequent to the time the Confidential Information was communicated to Recipient by Discloser through no fault of Recipient; (b) was rightfully in Recipient’s possession free of any obligation of confidence at or subsequent to the time the Confidential Information was communicated to Recipient by the Discloser; (c) was developed by employees or agents of Recipient independently of and without reference to any of Discloser’s Confidential Information; or (d) was communicated by Discloser to an unaffiliated third party free of any obligation of confidence. A disclosure by Recipient of any of Discloser’s Confidential Information (a) in response to a valid order by a court or other governmental body; (b) as otherwise required by law; or (c) necessary to establish the rights of either party under this Agreement will be considered a breach of this Agreement by the Recipient; provided, however, that Recipient provides prompt prior written notice to the Discloser to enable Discloser to seek a protective order or otherwise prevent the disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: The original text stated: 'A disclosure by Recipient of any of Discloser’s Confidential Information (a) in response to a valid order by a court or other governmental body; (b) as otherwise required by law; or (c) necessary to establish the rights of either party under this Agreement will not be considered to be a breach of this Agreement by the Recipient'. This creates a contradition, because the new text now states that the disclosure BY LAW of any confidential information, is now a breach of agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Recipient’s obligations under Section 2 will not apply to any of Discloser’s Confidential Information that Recipient can document: (a) was in the public domain at or subsequent to the time the Confidential Information was communicated to Recipient by Discloser through no fault of Recipient; (b) was rightfully in Recipient’s possession free of any obligation of confidence at or subsequent to the time the Confidential Information was communicated to Recipient by the Discloser; (c) was developed by employees or agents of Recipient independently of and without reference to any of Discloser’s Confidential Information; or (d) was communicated by Discloser to an unaffiliated third party free of any obligation of confidence. A disclosure by Recipient of any of Discloser’s Confidential Information (a) in response to a valid order by a court or other governmental body; (b) as otherwise required by law; or (c) necessary to establish the rights of either party under this Agreement will be considered a breach of this Agreement by the Recipient; provided, however, that Recipient provides prompt prior written notice to the Discloser to enable Discloser to seek a protective order or otherwise prevent the disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 3 to state that the disclosure of confidential information under specific circumstances (court order, legal requirement, or establishing rights) is considered a breach of the agreement. This directly contradicts the original text, which stated that such disclosure would 'not be considered to be a breach'. The contradicted text is the changed text, thus creating an in-text contradition by conflicting with the original version of itself.

perturbation.original_text: 6. This Agreement will become effective as of the Effective Date and will continue until the date one party receives written notice of termination of this Agreement from the other party; provided, however, that a Recipient’s obligations under Section 2 will survive termination of this Agreement and will continue with respect to the Discloser’s Confidential Information until the obligations no longer apply pursuant to Section 3 above.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. This Agreement will become effective as of the Effective Date and will continue until the date one party receives written notice of termination of this Agreement from the other party; provided, however, that a Recipient’s obligations under Section 2 will NOT survive termination of this Agreement and will continue with respect to the Discloser’s Confidential Information until the obligations no longer apply pursuant to Section 3 above.
perturbation.explanation: The original text stated: 'provided, however, that a Recipient’s obligations under Section 2 will survive termination of this Agreement', while the changed text now states: 'provided, however, that a Recipient’s obligations under Section 2 will NOT survive termination of this Agreement'. The original text implies the obligations defined in Section 2, will still apply, even after the agreement has been terminated. The new text, implies that the terms are no longer valid and the agreement is fully terminated with no obligations after termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. This Agreement will become effective as of the Effective Date and will continue until the date one party receives written notice of termination of this Agreement from the other party; provided, however, that a Recipient’s obligations under Section 2 will NOT survive termination of this Agreement and will continue with respect to the Discloser’s Confidential Information until the obligations no longer apply pursuant to Section 3 above.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 6 directly contradicts itself. Initially, the text states that the agreement continues until a party receives written notice of termination. However, it includes a provision regarding the recipient's obligations under Section 2. The original text indicated that these obligations would survive termination, while the changed text states the opposite: 'will NOT survive termination.' The contradictory statement that the recipient's obligations both will and will not survive termination within the same section, creates a clear in-text contradiction as defined by 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 9. Any notice required or permitted by this Agreement will be in writing and will be delivered as follows, with notice deemed given as indicated: (a) by personal delivery, when delivered personally; (b) by overnight courier, upon written verification of receipt; or (c) by certified or registered mail, return receipt requested, upon verification of receipt. Notice will be sent to the addresses set forth above or to such other address as either party may provide in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. Any notice required or permitted by this Agreement will be in writing and will be delivered as follows, with notice deemed given as indicated: (a) by personal delivery, when delivered personally; (b) by overnight courier, upon written verification of receipt; or (c) by email, with confirmation email. Notice will be sent to the addresses set forth above or to such other address as either party may provide in writing. Further more, by verbal communication.
perturbation.explanation: The original text has 3 ways of comunication: a) By personal delivery, b) Overnight courier, c) Certified or registered mail. Meanwhile the new text, added and introduced a new way of comunication, by verbal comunication. Which contrats the original text that mentions that all comunication must be in writing.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Any notice required or permitted by this Agreement will be in writing and will be delivered as follows, with notice deemed given as indicated: (a) by personal delivery, when delivered personally; (b) by overnight courier, upon written verification of receipt; or (c) by email, with confirmation email. Notice will be sent to the addresses set forth above or to such other address as either party may provide in writing. Further more, by verbal communication.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section 9 to include verbal communication as an acceptable form of notice. The original text and the contradicted text state that all notices must be in writing. Therefore, the addition of 'verbal communication' directly contradicts the requirement for written notice, creating a strong in-text contradiction.
